Summary: Mejoras en cuestionario “Solicitud de Transfusión”
Description:
**Problem** 
Valentina Arcos requested enhancements to the “Solicitud de Transfusión” questionnaire. She identified two key issues: 

1. When a hemocomponent is selected in the “Componente a transfundir” field, the other components and their associated laboratory exam fields should be automatically disabled to prevent erroneous entries. 
2. The printed transfusion request must display the name of the selected hemocomponent. 

A supporting document (“Formato ER.docx”) was uploaded to justify the request. The issue was initially transferred to Chile Support and then to Gabriela Pizarro, who began the investigation and created JIRA ticket TC‑413005 to track the request.

**Solution** 
The Product team reviewed the request and determined that the proposed changes are not feasible within the current system architecture. Key findings: 

- **Field Blocking**: The system cannot prevent a professional from entering a lab value that is available at the time. Leaving a field blank is the only way to avoid recording a value, and if multiple hemocomponents are requested, the logic to disable unrelated fields would not hold. 
- **Report Title**: The title of the printed request cannot be dynamically altered based on the selected hemocomponent. 
- **Mandatory Fibrinogen**: The requirement to record a fibrinogen value cannot be removed. Users who do not wish to enter a value can instead use the existing “No Aplica/Sin Resultado” checkbox beside the fibrinogen field. 

Arcos was informed that the requested blocking of fields and dynamic report naming are not possible. She was advised to communicate these constraints to the requesters and decide how to proceed. No further action items were identified beyond the communication of this limitation.

**Additional Information** 
- The JIRA ticket TC‑413005 moved from “Logged” to “Open,” then to “Closed” after the Product response. 
- Gabriela Pizarro remained available for comments and confirmed that the ticket would be closed once stakeholders accepted the solution. 
- Vera Guzmán, a system user, acknowledged the clarification, informed the relevant parties, and formally closed the support ticket. 
- No further tickets or follow‑up actions were initiated; the issue was considered resolved from the support standpoint.

Summary: HPE - Respuestas por defecto (EX S394187)
Description:
**Problem** 
The client (Matías Fuenzalida) needed to remove the answer option “Aspirado Nasofaríngeo” (shown in red) from four questions in the form and set “Hisopado Nasofaríngeo” as the default response. The request was submitted via an ER document (PC-Q0049). InterSystems support acknowledged the priority and forwarded the ticket to the Chile support team. The ER document and the request were transferred to Maria José Arroyo for analysis. Maria José informed the client that the response options had already been altered in the UAT environment (ticket SR S394187) but the default value had not been set because the ER document still requested the removal of the four options. The client attached an image of the ER document to emphasize the need for a default answer.

Subsequent communications highlighted that the product team determined the requested default response could only be set at the project level and that leaving the questions unanswered is technically unfeasible. However, the client was willing to accept the removal of the four questions and the automatic setting of the sample type as a workaround.

**Solution** 
Implementation engineer Carolina Tapia evaluated the ER document and applied the proposed configuration in a test environment. The solution was to delete the four questions and configure the default sample type to “Hisopado Nasofaríngeo” for each remaining question. After the client reviewed the proposal, they accepted it. The configuration was then deployed to UAT. Validation in UAT failed initially because the questions did not appear, but after a second review, the configuration was confirmed to match the acceptance criteria (questions removed and default sample type in place). The changes were subsequently applied to the production environment. Final validation in LIVE succeeded, and the TRC was closed by the client.

**Additional Information** 
- Two JIRA tickets (TC‑413511 and TC‑418125) were created and closed as part of the process. 
- The product team documented that a default answer must be set at the project level and that the original request (to set a default answer without prior response) was not technically possible; hence the removal of questions was the chosen workaround. 
- The ER document was revised and approved by the client after the second review. 
- All changes were tracked through the ticketing system, and final confirmation was obtained from the client before closing the support request.

Summary: Intermittent issue with questionnaire ENNTPADAV2
Description:
Problem 
The live ENNTPADAV2 “Post‑admission Consultant Ward Round” questionnaire intermittently displays sequential numbers instead of free‑text entered by clinicians. The incorrect numbers appear on the main display, but the correct text can be seen when editing or in side‑panel forms. Clinicians report that the issue can occur on the same machine, same patient, same day, and then disappear the next time the questionnaire is completed. Attempts to replicate the bug in live or non‑live environments have failed. Ben Marshall has attached screenshots and a doc outlining the exact steps clinicians take, but no consistent pattern has emerged. IT has examined performance logs between 8‑10 am, checked local PCs, and reviewed software variances with no findings. The problem seems user‑dependent, but no user error has been identified.

Solution 
Mark Ryan investigated audit logs and discovered that the problem occurs when a clinician updates the questionnaire via the Active Clinical Note (ACN) but then, while still busy, moves the focus away from the ACN screen. When they later update the questionnaire directly through the EPR, the Encounter record isn’t refreshed with the latest data, leaving the questionnaire out of sync and causing the sequential number display. Because the questionnaire update was not recorded in the audit trail, the incident was hard to trace. Ryan concluded the issue was a sync problem between ACN and the questionnaire record and marked the ticket resolved. No code changes or system patches were required; the fix is procedural: ensure clinicians finish all updates within the ACN before switching to the questionnaire interface.

Action Items 
- Ben is asked to close the ticket once confirming the resolution. 
- Ben has been notified that the ticket will be closed automatically in 30 days if no reply. 
- No further technical actions are pending; the root cause was user workflow rather than a software defect. 

Additional Information 
- The ticket was originally opened as “Incident: Live” but later changed to “Incident: NonSLA” for reporting. 
- Vinni Khurana raised a question about a CCR that had created a v3 of the questionnaire; Ryan clarified that only v2 exists in BASE and LIVE, so the CCR is unrelated. 
- Screenshots of the sequential numbers and the steps taken were submitted in “Post Consultant questionnaire steps.docx.” 
- The audit review highlighted missing entries when updates were made outside ACN, explaining why the issue is intermittent and tied to clinician focus. 
- The ticket was passed through several support layers: UK 2nd Line, Amy Morant, Mark Ryan, and Vinni Khurana, before final resolution.

Summary: Audit on questionnaires
Description:
Problem 
Tricia asked whether clinicians can see who edited a questionnaire and what changes were made. The request was forwarded to Jeff for investigation.

Solution 
Jeff directed Tricia to use the built‑in audit feature. Steps: 
1. Go to **Tools > Auditing > Table Audit**. 
2. Enter the date range for the audit. 
3. In the table field, type the questionnaire table name in the format **questionnaire.Qxxx**, where “xxx” is the questionnaire code (e.g., for the Intravascular Access questionnaire GXXXIVA, enter **questionnaire.QGXXXIVA**). 
A screenshot of the process was shared via Teams.

Additional Information 
Luke from support followed up to confirm whether any further help was needed before closing the ticket. Tricia confirmed the issue was resolved and closed the ticket. No additional actions were required.

  2. Douleur – DN4 FRXXSC0023 (Edition Française – Rééducation, Scores – Tous) 
  3. Douleur – DAN FRXXSC0026 (Néonat – douleur, Edition Française – Rééducation, Scores – Tous, SSR – Questionnaires IDE-AS). 
- The disappearance occurs because the questionnaire groups are configured at the regional level; during site updates, items added at the site level are omitted. 
- Caroline Dachary wants a permanent solution so these questionnaires persist across future upgrades.

**Solution** 
- Zineb Mabrouk added the missing questionnaires to the group “Edition Française – Rééducation” and confirmed visibility using the “réeduc” group. 
- She created a post‑install action to re‑add the items automatically after each upgrade. 
- She noted that the configuration is a site‑level setting on regional elements, so they will be skipped during updates. 
- A systematic post‑upgrade task was documented to ensure the changes are applied every time. 
- Mabrouk communicated with R&D to investigate a more permanent approach. 
- The team explored the possibility of creating site‑level questionnaire groups; experts clarified that the “Groupe de questionnaires” table is only SYSTEM or REGION level, so site‑level groups cannot be created. 
- Consequently, the solution relies on the post‑install/action task to keep the questionnaires in QLF.

**Additional Information** 
- The ticket moved through several support channels (French support, Mabrouk/Zineb, Rjiba, Enrica Fossa). 
- Mabrouk kept the ticket open while awaiting R&D input; Enrica later closed the ticket due to no further comments. 
- No new questions were received from Caroline after the last update, leading to the closure. 
- Future actions: monitor the post‑install task after each upgrade, confirm persistence, and if needed, revisit the design to avoid the regional‑only limitation.

Summary: Adding shift assessment to intravascular assessment questionnaire fails in LIVE
Description:
**Problem** 
The intravascular access questionnaire (component GXXXIVA) in the LIVE environment fails to allow any user to add a shift assessment. When the “Add” button is clicked within any accordion, a pop‑up error reads *“Staff: does not have a valid entry”*. The error is reproducible in LIVE for all tested users, but cannot be replicated in TEST, PRODSUP, SCRATCH, or other non‑LIVE environments. All users who tried the operation encountered the same issue. The workaround that staff are currently using is to leave the Staff field empty before clicking Add, which bypasses the error but is not a viable long‑term solution. The problem appears to have started after the 2023.5 AUBH ADHOC6 deployment to LIVE. 

**Solution** 
Jeff Malvar investigated and found that regenerating the questionnaire in LIVE resolved the issue. Using the terminal command 
```
s stat=##class(web.SSUserDefWindow).webGenerate("139","","",0,"",1)
``` 
re‑compiled the questionnaire. After regeneration, the “Add” operation worked without the error. However, compile errors were still logged in LIVE (none in BASE or PRODSUP). The regeneration is considered the effective fix for now. An RCA was created and linked to the incident. The ticket was closed once the fix was verified by multiple staff and the workaround was no longer needed. 

**Additional Information** 
- The issue was reported by Damien Hurrell, transferred to ANZ triage, then to Jeff Malvar. 
- Multiple staff across several departments reported the error, prompting a follow‑up from Elyse Adam and Marvin Angelo Sunaz. 
- Paul Collins suggested forcing script regeneration or generating the questionnaire from the terminal to avoid timeouts. 
- A test patient (UR 555556) in LIVE was used for debugging. 
- The error is tied to the “Staff” field referencing CT_CareProv; no similar issues exist in other environments. 
- The error does not occur when the questionnaire is updated as a whole, only when adding a shift assessment. 
- The RCA confirms the root cause is a script/compile issue introduced in the latest ad hoc release. 
- All stakeholders (Jeff, Damien, Marvin, Paul, Elyse, and Luke) confirmed the fix and closed the ticket after the issue was resolved and an RCA was documented.

Summary: Questionnaire Move to live.
Description:
Problem 
The Clinical Pathway questionnaire (Code: MECACPFSP) for surgeries—Hernia Repair, Appendectomy, and Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy—needs to be activated for live use. Paolo, Mon submitted a request via the InterSystems iSelfService system, specifying the priority and requesting that the questionnaire be moved to live status. The ticket was initially logged and assigned a priority, but the exact steps and verification needed were not immediately clear to the requester. 

Solution 
Jennifer from the ISC team confirmed receipt of the ticket and indicated that the issue was being investigated. The ticket was then routed to the ME Triage group and subsequently to ME Clinical Support, represented by Nermin Lahloh. Nermin first consulted the product team to ensure no dependencies or conflicts existed before proceeding. Once cleared, Nermin executed the deployment of the questionnaire to the live environment. He confirmed the successful move and communicated the completion back to the requester, advising a validation step and a note about a specific UI requirement: the “Open in full screen” checkbox must be selected at the encounter entry action level. 

The key action items completed:
- Questionnaire MECACPFSP transferred from staging to live.
- Confirmation of successful deployment sent to Paolo, Mon.
- Instruction provided to validate the questionnaire in the live environment.
- Instruction to check the “Open in full screen” checkbox during encounter entry actions.

Additional Information 
- No additional troubleshooting steps were reported; the deployment was straightforward after product confirmation.
- The ticket was closed by Paolo, Mon after acknowledging the support and confirming the questionnaire was live.
- Documentation of the checkbox requirement was attached by Nermin to ensure the UI functions as expected in production.
- No further follow-up or escalation was noted; the ticket is considered resolved.

Summary: Allowance for Natural Death (AOND) Form Signature Label Updates
Description:
**Problem** 
Arun Das requested a change to the labels of the signature fields on the AOND form, citing documentation attached as “Allowance for Natural Death.jpg.” The core issue was to modify the description of a lookup field within the questionnaire. Because altering this lookup could impact the history of past data, Arun clarified that the form was new and would only be used moving forward. The ticket was initially created in InterSystems iSelfService and transferred through several handlers (Haggerty, Lahloh, Arora, Silva) before reaching the appropriate functional owner, Roberta Silva.

**Solution** 
Roberta Silva confirmed that the requested changes had been implemented in the TRAIN1 environment. Arun tested the updated form and verified that the labels appeared as intended, reporting no issues. After validation, Arun scheduled a Business Change Control Board (BCCB) presentation on the 20th of the month, anticipating approval for a live release on the 21st night. The BCCB approval was obtained, and Arun announced that the move to live could proceed after 10 pm. Roberta reiterated that the changes were complete and asked for final confirmation. Arun responded with a brief acknowledgment.

**Additional Information** 
- The change involved editing a lookup field’s description; no other parts of the form were affected. 
- Environment sequence: Development → TRAIN1 (validation) → Approval (BCCB) → Live deployment. 
- Key contacts: Arun Das (requester), Jennifer Haggerty (initial triage), Nermin Lahloh (ME change request), Mandeep Singh Arora (Pontes, Erica), Roberta Silva (functional owner). 
- Timeline: Issue raised on the date of the first message, validation completed shortly after, BCCB meeting on the 20th, live release planned for the 21st night. 
- No additional technical or compliance concerns were raised beyond the initial note about historical data. 
- The ticket was logged in the InterSystems iSelfService system, indicating priority and escalation procedures were followed.

Summary: Questionnaire failed in TEST
Description:
**Problem** 
Andrew Snowdon reported a transaction error when attempting to save the completed questionnaire “Post‑admission consultant ward round (Adult) v3” (ENNTPADAV3) on the TEST environment. The error occurs only after data is entered into the form fields. The issue is limited to newly migrated questionnaires (v3 and ENNTCCHO) and does not affect existing ones. Initial troubleshooting steps included re‑downloading the item set, creating a change session for CCR ENNT5328, and regenerating the questionnaire. None of these actions resolved the error. The Spyglass lookup for the “Lead” doctor field fails to validate or return results, and the lookup appears non‑functional across the new questionnaires. Tests were performed in Microsoft Edge’s compatibility mode; Spyglass was also non‑responsive in other questionnaires on the same side of the mirror.

**Solution** 
Debbie Falce identified the root cause as a mis‑matched environment: the team was testing on the wrong side of the mirror in the TEST system. She logged into the correct side (http://ennt2020testtc02.xnth.nhs.uk/trakcare/) and verified that questionnaire creation, Spyglass lookup, and updates succeeded without error. Jessica Robinson confirmed the fix after retesting on the correct side; the transaction error disappeared and the Spyglass functionality worked. Consequently, the ticket was closed.

**Additional Information** 
- The incident was escalated to the UK Support Team and re‑classified from “Non Live” to “NonSLA” (customer‑owned) to reflect the mirror‑environment issue. 
- The affected questionnaires include ENNTPADAV3 and ENNTCCHO, both newly migrated to TEST. Existing questionnaires did not exhibit the problem. 
- The environment details: Security Group = ENXX Doctors Inpatient; Access Profile = ENNT EPMA Doctor; Front‑end uses “Adult Admission” encounter type. 
- The team is updating the environment list to ensure all users test on the correct mirror side. 
- The Spyglass lookup issue may be isolated to new questionnaires on the incorrect side; once switched, it functions normally. 
- The ticket was resolved after confirming functionality on the correct side and closing the incident.

Summary: screen for patient specific functionality scale
Description:
**Problem** 
Omer Mohamed reported an issue with the patient‑specific functional scale screen. He attached a snapshot and asked for two specific items: (1) alignment of numbers on the form so that highlighted numbers appear in a sequence, and (2) configuration of the security and access profile for the “therapist” role under the Rehabilitation security group. The ticket was initially logged as an incident but was later shifted between incident and service‑request states by the team. The root of the confusion lies in whether this is a bug that requires a development change or a standard configuration change. Nermin Lahloh asked for clarification on the alignment requirement and the security profile, noting that this could be a change request needing approval. Mohamed updated the snapshot with the numbers to be aligned and repeatedly requested the ticket to be marked “in progress” while awaiting the upcoming release T2023.6. Rahul Malik confirmed that the layout issue had been fixed in release T2023.6 and suggested that the ticket should be closed, with follow‑up on the original ticket.

**Solution** 
- The alignment of the numbers is now documented in Mohamed’s latest snapshot. The development team has confirmed that the layout change is fixed in release T2023.6. 
- Security and access profile: the request concerns assigning the “therapist” role with access to the Rehabilitation security group. The exact permissions still need to be defined; Nermin indicates that this may require a formal change request. 
- Ticket status: Mohamed asked for the ticket status to change from “resolved” to “in progress” until the release is deployed. The ticket was switched back to a service request, and the status was left unchanged until further clarification. 
- Closing: Mohamed marked the current ticket for closure and stated he would follow up on the original ticket for any additional work.

**Additional Information** 
- The ticket has been moved multiple times: from Incident: Live → Service Request: Default Service Request → Incident: Live. 
- The issue was identified in the MEUI questionnaire layout, differing from the TCUI code table layout. 
- The solution was validated by Rahul Malik in JIRA, which linked the fix to release T2023.6. 
- Action items: 1) Define the required permissions for the therapist role within the Rehabilitation security group and formalise this as a change request if necessary. 2) Confirm the alignment of numbers against the latest snapshot and ensure it matches the release documentation. 3) Update the ticket status to “in progress” while awaiting deployment of T2023.6, then change to “resolved” once the deployment confirms the fix. 4) Continue monitoring the original ticket for any further configuration changes.

Summary: Un-enddating several codes in the ENXXSCIM Questionaire
Description:
**Problem** 
Eoin Sheils requested that the “Walks without walking aids” control response be removed from the end‑dated list for codes 19, 20, and 21 of the ENXXSCIM questionnaire (Mobility Indoors, Mobility for Moderate Distances, Mobility Outdoors). The change would effectively make the response active. Sheils asked whether this could be achieved through a Control Change Request (CCR), noting it would simply involve removing an end date.

The ticket (#420895) was initially opened by Sheils, transferred to UK Support. Dianne from the UK support team acknowledged the priority, forwarded the issue to the regional second‑line support team for further investigation, and later transferred the case back to Debbie for review. Debbie confirmed the issue and requested any additional information needed.

**Solution** 
Debbie explained that to alter the end‑dated status, one should create a CCR and then start a change session. Within that session, the end date of the relevant questionnaire fields can be edited to activate them. She recommended adding a future end date rather than completely removing the field, which would maintain data integrity and provide a rollback point if needed. No further complications were identified; Debbie indicated she would contact Sheils if any additional clarification was required.

**Additional Information** 
- Ticket reference: 420895. 
- Sequence of interactions: Sheils → UK Support (Dianne) → Second‑line regional team → Debbie. 
- Debbie’s key action: instruct Sheils to use CCR and change session to modify end dates; advise setting a future end date. 
- Sheils acknowledged Debbie’s guidance. 
- No outstanding tasks beyond executing the described CCR process; no additional follow‑up was requested.

Summary: Visual Rule creation
Description:
Problem 
Tricia needs instructions to create a visual rule that uses questionnaire BHDRREC. Requirements: the questionnaire must be in “Authorised” status, apply to the current inpatient, trigger only once per episode, and send an email notification. She attempted this in Scratch but cannot add the questionnaire status (e.g., “Authorised”) as a condition in the visual rule wizard. The lookup only shows the questionnaire status field but not its related value, indicating a cube generation/indexing issue. Usman initially suspects a cube indexing problem and requests investigation. Adrian reports that the build process is still running and CPU is pegged at 100% for large parts of the day, implying system unresponsiveness. After the build completes, Usman confirms that the SCR2 (Bendigo Scr environment) infrastructure appears normal, but the questionnaire still does not appear correctly in the visual rule. Luke points out that JIRA TC‑373871 (Visual Rule Condition does not trigger Questionnaires) may have fixed the issue in release T24.1, but the staging environment does not yet contain the questionnaire. Usman notes that once T24.1 is available in the relevant environment, the issue should be retested. Later, after SCR is refreshed from live, Usman asks Tricia to recreate the questionnaire and verify if the visual rule still fails.

Solution 
1. Verify that the questionnaire BHDRREC is present in the target environment and that the deepsee cube generation flag is checked. 
2. Rebuild and activate the analytic data cubes; confirm that the questionnaire appears in the visual rule wizard with all status values, including “Authorised.” 
3. If the questionnaire still fails to load values, check for CPU or memory bottlenecks during the cube build; ensure system resources are adequate. 
4. If the issue persists, reference JIRA TC‑373871 and deploy the fix from release T24.1 into the environment where the questionnaire is used. 
5. Once the environment contains the fixed release, test the visual rule again, ensuring it triggers on the correct conditions (current inpatient, authorised status, once per episode) and sends the email notification. 
6. Document the steps and any errors encountered for future troubleshooting.

Additional Information 
- The issue appears tied to cube indexing; no direct configuration in the visual rule wizard fixes it. 
- Adrian suggested waiting for the build to finish, but once completed the problem remained, indicating that the issue is not solely CPU-related. 
- Luke’s note on JIRA TC‑373871 implies that the visual rule condition bug may already be resolved in T24.1; the staging environment lacks the questionnaire, so the fix cannot be tested until the questionnaire is available. 
- Usman has attached a simple example for reference, but the core problem is the questionnaire status value not being selectable. 
- The ticket was eventually closed after Usman provided instructions and the questionnaire was recreated, indicating that the issue was resolved following the steps above.

Summary: T2023.6 : Waterlow Questionnaire (TCWATERLOw) can't be deprecated
Description:
Problem 
- Mercyascot relies heavily on the existing TCWATERLOW questionnaire for Nursing encounters and multiple reports (coroner, inpatient safety, etc.). 
- The form has been marked as deprecated, which would break current workflows if used during the 2023.7 release. 
- No JIRA ticket detailing the deprecation or a transition plan is found. 
- The team cannot adopt the 2023.7 release without a proper migration strategy. 
- The new questionnaire (TCWPUPS – Waterlow Score 2005) is intended to replace TCWATERLOW, but the exact version and rollout timeline are unclear. 
- Questions remain: Is TCWATERLOW still active? When and how will TCWPUPS be enabled? What is the status of the Waterlow Scale (GXXXWATERL) and its replacement?

Solution 
- Re‑activate TCWATERLOW temporarily to keep workflows operational while a transition plan is developed. 
- Perform testing on TCWPUPS to confirm it meets current clinical and reporting requirements before full cut‑over. 
- Document a phased migration strategy: keep TCWATERLOW active until TCWPUPS is fully validated, then deprecate TCWATERLOW officially. 
- Coordinate with Australian support (AUXX MASTER) and triage teams (ANZ Support/Triage) to ensure the new questionnaire and related accesses are enabled, following JIRA tickets TC-396482 and TC-397156. 
- Confirm that the new Waterlow questionnaire is indeed intended to replace the Waterlow Scale (GXXXWATERL) in the Australian solution, as referenced in TC-358766. 
- Update the release notes and inform all users of the upcoming changes, ensuring they are aware that “deprecated” means no further enhancements, not immediate removal.

Additional Information 
- The ticket was originally assigned to Kam, Enjee and later transferred to ANZ triage and support (Tan, Alesha). 
- Luke Evers and Alesha clarified that “deprecated” does not remove the form, only halts enhancements; the form can still be used. 
- Luke advised continuing to use TCWATERLOW while testing TCWPUPS. 
- The new questionnaire is slated to replace the old Waterlow Scale, but the exact release timeline remains unconfirmed. 
- Security context: AUXX MASTER; non‑live environment: SCR1 only. 
- Severity: all; no report letters attached. 
- The team must obtain a formal transition plan before proceeding with the 2023.7 release to avoid disrupting existing workflows.

Summary: Issues with questionnaire SCGCRHOAK Rehum Knee Assm in UAT
Description:
Problem 
The questionnaire in UAT began throwing error #5001 after Andrew saved a Text Rep change to site level. The error states that the questionnaire is not generated. Screenshots were provided and a document titled “Knee – read only error.doc” was uploaded. The ticket was assigned to UK Support. Amy Morant noted the error file (DF46E80E-32A8-11ED-9389-005056A74AC9.xml) was read‑only, preventing export. She forwarded the issue to Mark Ryan, who inspected the file’s permissions. The directory path is /trak/scgcUAT/tc/store/perforce/custom_ccrs/scx/SCGC/T2020/BASE/misc/websys/Component/. The file was not set to the required mode (‑rwxrw‑r--, octal 764), causing the 5001 error.

Solution 
Mark Ryan changed the permissions on the problematic XML file to 764, created a backup copy, and set the same permissions on the backup. Commands used: 
```
chmod 764 DF46E80E-32A8-11ED-9389-005056A74AC9.xml
cp DF46E80E-32A8-11ED-9389-005056A74AC9.xml DF46E80E-32A8-11ED-9389-005056A74AC9.xml.bak
chmod 764 DF46E80E-32A8-11ED-9389-005056A74AC9.xml.bak
``` 
After these changes, Ryan asked Lokesh to retry the questionnaire in UAT. Lokesh confirmed the issue was resolved. Ryan marked the ticket as Resolved and requested ticket closure confirmation. Multiple follow‑up messages were sent to ensure closure; if not confirmed, the ticket would be closed automatically within 30 days.

Additional Information 
- The root cause was file permission misconfiguration, not a bug in the questionnaire logic. 
- The issue was handled by UK 2nd Line support, with escalation to the 2nd Line team and then to Mark Ryan. 
- The ticket ID is 423623. 
- Future work requires ensuring proper permission settings on newly created or edited questionnaire components. 
- Users are advised to raise a new ticket referencing this one for any further assistance.

Summary: RIABILITAZIONE: Modifica questionario RRF
Description:
**Problem** 
Nadia from Assistenza TrakCare reported that the “questionario RRF” could not be edited or cancelled once validated in the live environment. She confirmed that the test environment allowed both actions when the user entered a reason for change or cancellation, but the corresponding fields were disabled in production. The specific case involved SDO 2024000243, which was scheduled for discharge today; the doctor needed to modify the questionnaire but was blocked. The ticket was opened by ServiceDeskASOAL and then escalated through Italy Support, Arcidiacono, and Russo. 

**Solution** 
Ilaria Arcidiacono explained that the ability to modify a finalized questionnaire depends on a user profile setting: the parameter “Può modificare un questionario definitivo.” She enabled this flag for the profile “DEGENZE - Reparto medico e direzione sanitaria.” Once the parameter was activated, the doctor was able to edit the questionnaire as required. Nadia confirmed that the SDO 2024000243 was now correctly discharged and the questionnaire was in the proper state. The ticket was then closed by Ilaria. 

**Additional Information** 
- The issue was initially flagged by ServiceDeskASOAL and routed to Italy Support for investigation. 
- Sara De Corato coordinated the escalation and monitored the verification process. 
- The problem was specific to the live environment; the test environment behaved as expected. 
- The key change involved enabling a configuration flag at the user profile level. 
- After the flag was enabled, the doctor could both modify and cancel the questionnaire in live, restoring the expected functionality. 
- Nadia reported the resolution and confirmed that no further action was required. 
- The ticket was formally closed by Ilaria after verifying the discharge status and questionnaire correctness.

Summary: Perda de tabular items
Description:
Problem 
The questionnaire “SCMLFCBO” on the Folha de Consumo BO action is repeatedly losing all tabular items after a user updates a line and then saves. In the latest case (patient 247696, episode I049347.24, action 01/02/2024 09:23–09:33), user André Oliveira edited a material line, saved, then changed the reception location. The nurse later saw that all items were missing. Audit logs show the logistics user deleted all items in the same minute, even though the questionnaire form was not open. This mirrors a previous ticket (TRC I417623) and indicates a systemic issue with multi‑window or multi‑tab operations.

Solution 
Leopoldo Costa introduced a change to prevent simultaneous opening of two questionnaire windows by modifying the link behaviour. UAT tests confirmed that clicking the link opens only one window; however, users can still duplicate the session via Ctrl‑click, tab duplication, or other methods, which can recreate the problem. The change covers the majority of accidental double‑open cases but does not stop intentional duplication or manual deletion. Manuel Marques clarified that there is no workaround for users who actively trigger the error; the responsibility lies with the user not to circumvent the intended single‑window workflow. Despite this limitation, the team considered the change the best available solution. The fix was deployed to Live through CCR HOSA1023, making it available for all users. Nuno Horta confirmed the live deployment and closed the ticket.

Additional Information 
- The issue is tied to opening multiple browser tabs/windows for the same questionnaire session. 
- Audit logs confirm the deletion timestamp matches the user’s update action, with no open form. 
- Users are instructed to avoid opening multiple windows for a single questionnaire; the change is an additional safeguard. 
- No new error messages or prompts were added to prevent data deletion; the system currently lacks a guard against manual deletion in duplicate windows. 
- The ticket was resolved and closed after the live deployment and confirmation from the user.

Summary: Change to existing questionnaire
Description:
Problem 
Holly Robertson requested guidance on how to safely modify an existing questionnaire used by a service. She had heard of past issues when changing questionnaire content and wanted to avoid compromising data integrity or results interpretation. The ticket was opened (Ticket426251) and routed to UK support.

Solution 
Debbie Falce clarified that any change to a questionnaire field retroactively affects all historical responses, potentially invalidating past data. Therefore, the recommended approach is to “end date” the current questionnaire and create a new questionnaire with a new version number, preserving the original for historical reference. This preserves data integrity and ensures that respondents cannot alter already‑submitted answers. Holly also asked whether the new questionnaire could be linked to the existing order item; Debbie confirmed that the same order item can be reused without needing a new one. The ticket was marked resolved after confirming these steps and Holly’s confirmation that the issue was resolved.

Additional Information 
- The ticket was initially acknowledged by Jennifer Haggerty of the UK 2nd‑Line support team. 
- Debbie’s communication emphasized that once a questionnaire is answered, it cannot be edited, reinforcing the need for version control. 
- After confirming the solution, Debbie closed the ticket and asked Holly to confirm closure, which she did.

Summary: 800516712X reports questionnaire DEMTPDS already exists but not in EPR
Description:
Problem 
A user attempted to create a Dementia PDS Dataset questionnaire (DEMTPDS) for patient 800516712X but received an error that a questionnaire already exists. The user reported that no previous version could be found in the EPR. The issue was logged as Ticket 427543 and passed to the UK 2nd‑Line support team. The user had tried adding the questionnaire in episode NR43 with the SJH Health Records Library role and as a System Admin, but the same error persisted. The user also tried the latest episode (12/12/2023) without success.

Solution 
Bree Parker, the assigned support engineer, examined the database and found a DEMTPDS entry dated 27/09/2023 linked to episode O0022878066. This episode had been cancelled with a cancellation reason, and because no status was set for the questionnaire, it was hidden from the EPR. Bree explained that questionnaires completed in cancelled episodes are not displayed unless a cancellation reason of “Administrative error” is applied. Bree proposed a workaround: set the episode to current, cancel it again with “Administrative error”, and then cancel it again normally; this would cause the questionnaire to become visible. After further investigation, Bree discovered that the questionnaire had no status. She updated the record to set QUESStatusDR to “Entered” (status 2). The questionnaire now appears in the patient’s record. The user confirmed visibility.

Additional Information 
- The ticket was transferred from Mike Bresnahan to Bree Parker, with initial acknowledgment by Mike and Mark. 
- Bree requested episode information and security group details; the user confirmed NR43 and also tried System Admin access. 
- Bree provided screenshots of the database query and status update. 
- The user attempted to reverse the cancellation (O0022878066) and cancel it as “Administrative error” but still faced the error until Bree updated the questionnaire status. 
- The incident was reclassified from a Service Request to an Incident (NonSLA). 
- The user (David Kinmond) verified the questionnaire visibility after Bree’s update. 
- The resolution hinges on correctly setting the questionnaire status in a cancelled episode or using the “Administrative error” cancellation reason to allow display.

Summary: Assessments challenges
Description:
**Problem** 
The NICU reported that the Central Line Assessment (CLABSI bundle) is missing from the electronic record for several patients, most notably URN 1555024. The assessment is only visible for the most recent days (e.g., 06‑14 March) while data from earlier dates (20‑28 Feb, 5‑17 Feb) is absent. Nurses create a new version of the form each day, yet the earlier entries do not appear when the assessment is searched. This missing documentation compromises compliance with infection‑control standards and hampers audit trails. The issue has been logged in InterSystems iSelfService (ticket I400933, I394071) and JIRA (TC‑355771). 

**Solution** 
An ad‑hoc fix (T2022.4.SFHR.ADHOC9/10) was developed to correct the display problem. The fix is currently available in the SFHR Scratch environment but not yet deployed to the live system. Yara (ME Clinical Support) will apply the patch once the live environment is updated. In the interim, the nursing team will add new rows to an existing central‑line form and verify that all entries are displayed; this will confirm that the data exists but is not shown. Once the live patch is applied, the upgrade will release the corrected view. No change to data entry procedures is required beyond normal daily updates. 

**Additional Information** 
- Ticket status: JIRA TC‑355771 moved from Logged to Closed after the ad‑hoc deployment. 
- The issue was also linked to earlier tickets (I400933, I394071). 
- The problem only affects the UI; the underlying data remains intact. 
- Nurses access the form via EPR → Clinical Assessment → Assessment Forms → Doctor Assessment. 
- After the upgrade, a follow‑up check confirmed that recently added assessment forms display correctly. 
- No further action is pending; the environment will be patched soon, and the issue is resolved.

Summary: T2023.6 Synthèse de sortie CHAM manque un item
Description:

In T2023.6 the exit summary has been revised to include additional questions that differ for children and adults (issue I405053). In the section “Bilan de l'autonomie, soins infirmiers et rééducation” the field for meal intake does not display the “adapted to age” option. Kromer, Sandy reports screenshots and requests remote access to resolve the omission. The ticket is initially opened by Sandy and then escalated through the support hierarchy: from French Support (Dianne Chagaris) to other specialists (Inès Rjiba, Enrica Fossa, Jean‑Marc Ferrandis, Gaelle Petit, Christian Ambrosino, and a user identified as SILA). Multiple attempts to regenerate questionnaire FR04IP0003 on the CHAM TRACK BASE environment fail, and the problem persists on both the French edition and the test environment. The issue is logged as JIRA bug TC‑426615 and the ticket priority is increased from medium to high. 

**Solution** 
Jean‑Marc Ferrandis leads the investigation. He attempts to fix the issue directly in the T2023.6 environment but cannot resolve it. An RCA is created and the bug TC‑426615 is moved to ticket P244025 for tracking. Ferrandis commits to providing updates once progress is made, but no concrete fix is delivered within the conversation. The ticket is ultimately marked “Closed” after the bug is transferred to P244025, indicating that the issue will be addressed under that JIRA item. 

**Additional Information** 
- The conversation includes repeated status updates: “Analysis in progress on environment CHAM TRACK BASE in T2023.6,” “Still under investigation,” and “I will keep you posted.” 
- The JIRA status transitions are documented: TC‑426615 logged → open → tracked in P244025. 
- Multiple support staff are referenced: Dianne Chagaris (initial response), Inès Rjiba, Enrica Fossa, Jean‑Marc Ferrandis, Gaelle Petit, Christian Ambrosino, and SILA. 
- Screenshots were attached to the original ticket but not referenced later. 
- No final resolution or code patch is shared in the transcript; the issue remains open under a separate JIRA ticket.

Summary: Review Peripheral Line Questionnaire
Description:
**Problem** 
Mon reported that the “Peripheral Line” questionnaire no longer drops into the work area after being updated in the live environment. Initially this was documented in a video file titled *Peripheral Line does not drop in the work area once updated*. Mon indicated that the issue also occurs in the live environment, whereas it previously worked correctly in a non‑live setting. The problem appears to be linked to the difference between the “Add Peripheral Line Assessment” action (action 12) and the “Review Peripheral Line” action (action 2). The former creates a new questionnaire instance each time it is invoked, leading to duplicate questionnaire creation, while the latter is intended for reviewing existing entries. Mon asked why the functionality works in non‑live environments and how to replicate the issue.

**Solution** 
Nermin from the ME Clinical Support team clarified that the behavior is by design: the “Review Peripheral Line” action should replace “Add Peripheral Line Assessment” to prevent duplicate questionnaire instances. He provided guidance that after completing a questionnaire, the user must tick the “Attach Encounter Item” checkbox in the Peripheral Line Assessment Documentation section to add the entry to the encounter. Nermin also instructed Mon to attach the questionnaire in the non‑live environment following the same steps so the team could verify the replication. The ticket was moved from an Incident to a Service Request (Default Service Request) for proper handling. The resolution involves updating user instructions and ensuring the correct action is used.

**Additional Information** 
- Ticket number I295147 was created to address the issue and is linked to the original problem. 
- Action 19 from the other ticket advises adding the questionnaire to the entry via “attach to entry” and then editing it. 
- The team confirmed that the system’s current functionality is not a bug but a feature requiring correct workflow usage. 
- Mon was advised to close the ticket once the guidance is accepted and to use the “Review Peripheral Line” entry moving forward. 
- No further technical changes were required; the solution is purely procedural. 
- The conversation included a request for a detailed replication in the non‑live environment, which Mon provided. The issue is considered resolved after confirming the correct workflow.

Summary: T2023.6 soins synthèse pédiatrie V2
Description:
**Problem** 
Kromer/Sandy requested that new questions be added to the “SOINS – Synthèse entrée pédiatrie” questionnaire. The submitted questions were instead inserted into a V2 version of the questionnaire. The support team noted that the layout for this questionnaire was set at the site level, which prevented the new questions from being displayed in the base environment. Consequently, the questionnaire did not reflect the requested fields for the pediatric entry summary, and users could not see the updates in the “synthèse de sortie” tab.

**Solution** 
Inès Rjiba received the ticket and immediately applied the V2 questionnaire to the base (BASE) environment. She asked the users to verify that all fields matched their expectations. Gaelle Petit confirmed that the “pose du bracelet” and “contrôle d'identité” fields appeared correctly, indicating that the V2 questionnaire was properly deployed in the base. Rjiba then identified the root cause: a layout that was defined at the site level, overriding the regional layout and thus ignoring the newly added fields. She removed the site‑level layout and restored the regional layout, which restored the questionnaire to its intended configuration. After the layout was corrected, the questionnaire now displays all required fields and aligns with the V2 version requested by Kromer/Sandy.

**Additional Information** 
- The ticket was transferred from James Sheehan (French Support) to Inès Rjiba for resolution. 
- An attachment “Soins synthèse pédiatrie V2.docx” was uploaded with the original questionnaire specifications. 
- Rjiba confirmed the restoration of the questionnaire at the regional level and closed the ticket after Gaelle’s confirmation that the key fields (“pose du bracelet” and “contrôle d'identité”) were visible. 
- No further action items remain; the issue is resolved and the ticket is closed.

Summary: HFBC ETE Caprini boton previsualizar no hace nada
Description:
**Problem** 
A non‑functional “PREVISUALIZAR” button in the ETE CAPRINI questionnaire is confusing users. The button appears but performs no action. Rodrigo Pizarro reported the issue and attached a document (SSMOC_PC-Q0079 HFBC ETE Caprini boton previsualizar no hace nada.docx) showing the problem. The ticket (429161) was routed through InterSystems support, then to Chile L3, and finally to the implementation team.

**Solution** 
The implementation team investigated the button issue and created a JIRA ticket (CLTECH-4422) to track the fix. They determined that the questionnaire was generating an automatic simple signature in addition to fields for a handwritten signature, which was causing the “PREVISUALIZAR” button to appear useless. A change was made to remove or disable the redundant signature field, thereby restoring the intended behavior of the button. The fix was deployed to the UAT environment where Rodrigo Pizarro validated the functionality; the button now operates correctly and the signature fields display as expected (simple signature only). Following UAT approval, the change was moved to the Live environment for final validation. The production deployment is complete and the button works as intended.

**Additional Information** 
- The ticket was prioritized and tracked through multiple support levels: initial support, Chile support, and L3 implementation. 
- A document (SSMOC_PC-Q0079) and a JIRA ticket (CLTECH-4422) were uploaded to document the issue and the solution. 
- A link to the UAT view (https://ccr.intersystems.com/ccr/ng/view/SMOC4556) was provided for validation. 
- The issue was resolved and the change is now live; no further action is required from the user side.

Summary: Questionnaire: SCXXOSCC field content changing
Description:
**Problem** 
- In the SCXXOSCC questionnaire (SCXX – OSCC Day of Operation), the Cataract Surgery lookup field has three control responses. The second and third responses share the same description (“Right Phaco Cataract Surgery with Intraocular Lens Implant”). 
- Users can change the description of the second response to “Not Performed” locally. When the letter is previewed in an “In Progress” state, the correct “Not Performed” text is shown. 
- Once the letter is authorised, the field reverts to “Right Phaco Cataract Surgery with Intraocular Lens Implant” and this text is sent to the GP practice. The underlying questionnaire data does not change, so the problem is not a data entry bug but a rendering issue tied to the description change. 
- The problem appears across all Trakcare environments (Base, Upgrade, Live) except Upgrade, where the duplicate descriptions originally existed. 
- Attempts to re‑generate the questionnaire after changing the control responses failed; the authorisation step still forces the old text. 

**Solution** 
- The root cause was identified as a core issue with how updated questionnaire control‑response descriptions are rendered in authorised reports. This was fixed in JIRA TC‑389764, released in T2024.5. 
- An ad‑hoc patch (T2024.5) was applied to the relevant environments (Adhoc 6). After re‑generating the questionnaire, the “Not Performed” description remains correct in the authorised letter. 
- The earlier JIRA TC‑428261 addressed duplicate descriptions but did not resolve the rendering problem. TC‑389764 is the definitive fix.

Summary: Image path for questionnaires
Description: **Problem** Graham Hynam requested the correct image path to use in a questionnaire when the image has been uploaded into the Picture code table. The images are stored in the database (GlobalBinaryStream) rather than on the file system, so the standard `` reference may not work. He also asked how to upload a new patient‑specific image that is not yet in TrakCare and then reference it from a questionnaire. The ticket was transferred through triage to ANZ support and involved multiple contributors (Luke Evers, Martin Thomas) who provided partial answers and requested a call to clarify. **Solution** - For **annotated images** (TrakCare images) no path is needed; simply add a control of type “Annotated Images” to the questionnaire layout. - For **patient‑specific images** add a control of type “Image.” Again, no path is required when the image is stored in the Picture code table. - For **TC system images** (icons that already exist in TrakCare), the path is `/trak/aubh/live/tc/web/images/webemr`. - For **new custom images** not yet in TrakCare, upload the file to the `scr/images/webem` folder (e.g., `therm.png`) and reference it in the layout with an expression: `w ""`. - These instructions apply only to site‑created questionnaires; system and region questionnaires cannot be modified. - Luke added a knowledge‑base entry detailing the exact syntax and path, clarifying that the path should include the site branch (e.g., `/trak/aubh/live/tc/web/images/webemr`) and that system images may change. **Additional Information** - Graham scheduled a demonstration call with Martin on 13/03 at 10:30 or 15:00; the call was held and Martin provided a live demo of how the Picture code table maps to questionnaires. - After the demo, Graham decided to close the ticket, noting a potential future need for FTP access to the images folder, which will be parked for now. - Luke marked the ticket as resolved and added the solution to the knowledge base. - No further action items remain, aside from the optional FTP request that has been deferred.

Summary: Cannot create a resuscitation plan questionnaire UR 102956
Description:
**Problem** 
Ben Sherwell reports that when attempting to add a new resuscitation plan for patient URN 102956 the system returns “patient already has an acute resus plan…”. No resuscitation icon appears in the EPR, so the old questionnaire cannot be located or voided. The user cannot therefore create a new questionnaire. Investigation reveals that the existing plan was added to a cancelled episode (I100002491) with the cancellation reason “Admitted in error”. For this cancellation reason the EPR setting *Allow Display of Questionnaire in EPR* is disabled, so any questionnaire attached to that episode is hidden. Consequently the questionnaire cannot be accessed to change its status to void, blocking creation of a new plan. The issue was logged and escalated through ANZ triage, support and senior engineers (Luke Evers, Jeff Malvar). A JIRA ticket (TC‑346174) had the same error scheduled for release T2022.3 ADHOC6. 

**Solution** 
After triage the team confirmed the user could add a new resuscitation questionnaire once the old one was voided. The root cause was addressed by enabling the EPR display settings for all cancellation reasons, specifically: 
- *Allow Display of Questionnaire in EPR* 
- *Allow Results to Show on EPR* 
- *Display Encounters Linked to Cancelled Episodes in the Encounter Record* 

These changes were implemented via ticket S430212 (“automatic cancelling of pre‑arrival episodes”). An update to the underlying questionnaire table was also executed: 
```sql
UPDATE %notrigger questionnaire.QAUXXARP
SET Q32='02'
WHERE QUESPAPatMasDR=101078 AND ID=25888;
``` 
This marks the questionnaire as voided. With the settings enabled, the questionnaire becomes visible again, allowing the user to void or replace it. The ticket was subsequently re‑closed by Luke Evers. 

**Additional Information** 
- Workflow: EPR → Plans and pathways → Shared Decision Making (TC.CF.ACP). 
- Configured questionnaires: Inpatient Resuscitation Plan and Do Not Resuscitate (both system QPs). 
- The patient’s questionnaire was created on 10/03/2024 by user TEStark in episode I10002491, which was cancelled at 13:28 with “Admitted in Error”. 
- The SQL query used to locate the questionnaire for the patient is: 
  ```sql
  SELECT Q32,* FROM questionnaire.QAUXXARP
  WHERE QUESPAPatMasDR='101078';
  ``` 
- Document “Issue resus plan void.docx” was uploaded by Ben. 
- The issue was moved between support personnel: Ben → ANZ Triage → ANZ Support → Luke Evers → Jeff Malvar → Luke Evers. 
- Final resolution included enabling display settings for all cancellation reasons and running the SQL void command, after which Ben confirmed the ability to add a new questionnaire.

Summary: TEST: Radiology Questions are not displaying in the correct order in EPR
Description:
**Problem** 
- Two new radiology questions were added to the ‘Order Category’ of CCR ENYH9405. 
- In BASE environment, questions display and order correctly on the Order screen and in Order Details in the EPR. 
- In TEST environment, questions display correctly on the Order screen but appear in the wrong order in the Order Details view. 
- The issue is limited to TEST; BASE is unaffected. 
- Jessica has provided screenshots and a Word document detailing the discrepancy. 

**Solution** 
- Bree Parker determined the problem is due to missing sequence numbers for the response answers in the CCR; without these, CCR cannot maintain order across environments. 
- Bree recommended applying an override: add the questions individually in each environment (TEST and LIVE) rather than progressing the CCR bundle. This preserves the GUID order. 
- After making the changes, the customer should raise a new incident and request that the Bundle queue be cleared. 
- Bree has followed up asking Jessica to confirm resolution and close the ticket. The ticket will be closed automatically after 30 days if no response is received. 

**Additional Information** 
- The ticket was transferred from UK 2nd Line support to internal team members (Amy Morant and Bree Parker). 
- Status updates: Ticket moved from Incident: Live → Incident: Non Live → Incident: NonSLA; now set to be closed. 
- No further action items pending other than the customer's confirmation of resolution. 
- Jessica was asked to perform the environment-specific changes and to re-open a new ticket if additional assistance is required.

Summary: Signature Box Logic
Description:
**Problem** 
A user selects “Apply” or “Update” on a questionnaire that contains a signature box. Once that action is performed, the “Clear” button for the signature box becomes disabled, preventing the user from removing an accidental or incorrect signature. The user, Eoin Markham, reported this behavior to support, citing cases where staff mistakenly signed and could not correct the entry before finalizing the questionnaire. The issue is considered an enhancement because it affects product functionality rather than a defect that can be fixed immediately.

**Solution** 
The ticket (iService Ticket 432378) was escalated from the initial support engineer to the regional 2nd‑line UK team. No technical resolution was found, and the support channel did not provide a workaround. The conversation then shifted to the product development side: the ticket was re‑classified as an “Enhancement Request.” Bree Parker, the Functional Matter Expert, was repeatedly asked to fill out a problem‑definition template to enable internal assessment. The template was attached to multiple emails, but no response from Eoin was received, so the information remained incomplete. After a series of identical messages from Parker requesting the template, the ticket was transferred to another functional SME, Vinni Khurana, to attempt to move the request forward. No further progress was made. Eventually, the ticket was closed by Eoin with the note “Closed. Change won’t happen,” indicating that the enhancement will not be implemented and no workaround was offered.

**Additional Information** 
- The ticket began as a standard support request, was later escalated to a UK support team, and then moved to a functional SME for product change consideration. 
- The support team communicated the ticket status via multiple email exchanges, all of which repeated the same request for a problem definition template. 
- No technical investigation or alternative solutions were proposed; the issue remained unresolved. 
- The final action taken was to close the ticket with the statement that the requested change will not occur. No implementation plan or timeline was provided. 
- Stakeholders involved: Eoin Markham (user), Jennifer Haggerty (UK support), Amy Morant (support escalation), Bree Parker (Functional Matter Expert), Vinni Khurana (secondary SME). 
- The ticket did not transition to a development backlog or receive any change‑management approval.

Summary: Patching ticket: Intravascular Access questionnaire
Description:
**Problem** 
- The IV Access questionnaire in the STAGING environment shows layout corruption when the “Peripheral Venous” option is selected: questions for other catheter types appear. This issue recurs after every patch, attributed to the layout editor. 
- The same questionnaire also fails in the BENDIGO STAGING/Scratch environments and was not functioning since the environment was created. It works correctly in AUXX. 
- The Edit History feature displays an extra “(Observation Item) DR” column, showing internal DR IDs rather than user‑readable values. 
- The Edit History hyperlink is inactive in STAGING and sometimes in TEST, preventing audit review. 
- The layout issue has been confirmed in LIVE after regeneration, but the Edit History problem remains across BASE, TEST, and STAGING. 
- Multiple JIRAs have been opened: TC‑425999 (edit history columns/links) and TC‑429841 (erroneous DR column). 

**Solution** 
- The questionnaire must be regenerated in the backend each time a patch is applied. A note has been added to the Bendigo patching Confluence page (T2024.1) to enforce this step. 
- JIRA TC‑429841 has been created to remove the DR column from Edit History for questionnaires with Observation Items; it is scheduled for release in T2024.4. 
- JIRA TC‑425999 addresses the inactive Edit History hyperlink and the column display issue; it remains in progress. 
- The layout issue has been fixed in STAGING after regeneration; Ben verified the chart summaries in LIVE are correct. 
- No changes to the questionnaire will be made until the T2024.4 release, so testing in STAGING can be deferred until the TEST environment is patched. 

**Additional Information** 
- Ben confirmed the layout is correct in LIVE but still flagged the Edit History column issue. 
- Marvin observed the Edit History breaks when the questionnaire is reopened or updated, consistent across TEST and LIVE. 
- Luke and Evers are monitoring JIRA status; TC‑429841 is Open and will be resolved in T24.4, while TC‑425999 remains In Progress. 
- The decision has been made to hold further STAGING testing until the TEST environment receives the T2024.4 patch, at which point the Edit History behavior should be verified. 
- The patching process requires manual regeneration of the questionnaire in each environment; this procedure is now documented to prevent recurrence.

Summary: Tabular items novos subrepostos ao gravar - perda de dados
Description:
**Problem** 
- Nurses in the operating block reported that when they insert multiple rows into the tabular item of questionnaire QSCMFCBO (QSCMLFCBOQQ11), the rows disappear after the form is closed and reopened. 
- Logs show an “insert” followed immediately by an “update” of the same record within the same second – impossible manually. 
- The loss occurs on different PCs, with different users, since the system first inserts new rows, then later treats some of them as updates to existing rows, overwriting or deleting them. 
- The behaviour is specific to QSCMLFCBO but similar cases exist in other tabular items. 

**Solution** 
1. **Root cause** – In the auto‑generated `webHtmlAdd` method of the questionnaire class, the child sub‑identifier is calculated as 
   ``` 
   if id="" s id=$o(^CacheTemp.tcData(%session.SessionId,"QSCMLFCBO",parref,"Q11",""),-1)+1 
   ``` 
   This can collide with an ID already used for another response, causing an overwrite. 
2. **Initial workaround** – A custom JavaScript (`ChildAdd_Q11`) was added to invoke the “Save” button immediately after an add/edit so that each row is persisted before another is inserted.  This prevents the collision but forces a screen refresh. 
3. **Improved fix** – Implemented an `OnBeforeSave` User‑Defined Function (UDF) for all questionnaires containing tabular items. 
   * The UDF iterates over the temporary cache, detects duplicate child‑sub values, and re‑numbers them with a safe offset before the data is written to the database. 
   * This approach removes the need for automatic saving on every add/edit, preserving user experience. 
4. **Deployment** – 
   * The UDF was created in UAT, tested (no regressions), and then released live (CCR HOSA1033). 
   * The same UDF creation routine was run in the SAMS environment, creating UDFs for 24 questionnaire classes. 
5. **Verification & monitoring** – 
   * JIRA issue TC‑427495 opened for core correction; a future patch will address the underlying code. 
   * The live fix was verified by re‑running the problematic scenarios; no further data loss was observed. 
   * Users were informed that the “auto‑save” feature for QSCMLFCBO is back to the original behaviour but the new UDF prevents similar issues system‑wide. 

**Additional Information** 
- The fix was only applied to the live environment after UAT approval. 
- The UDF logic also logs changes to `^zleop` for audit purposes. 
- The earlier “auto‑save” customisation was disabled after the UDF was deployed to avoid double‑saving and unnecessary screen refreshes. 
- Future monitoring will focus on detecting any new instances of child‑sub collisions, using the UDF’s audit logs.

Summary: Data from previous questionnaire copied to new questionnaire >28 days old
Description:
**Problem** 
The Admission Assessment questionnaire is configured to pull data only from the last 28 days. However, a questionnaire created for a patient on 26/03/2024 mistakenly displayed data from an admission dated 28/07/2022. The issue was first reported by Sarah Emerson (Ticket #INC-95992). Initial checks confirmed that the configuration was correct. The root cause was identified as a user (LeighH) updating an existing questionnaire dated 28/07/2022 on 26/03/2024. Updating the original record changes its timestamp to the update date, so subsequent new questionnaires reference the most recent record rather than the intended 28‑day window. The new record created by MikaylaJ on 26/03/2024 therefore pulled the data from the updated 2022 record. The lack of data in the top fields of the old questionnaire likely misled the user into updating rather than creating a new one.

**Solution** 
Jeff Malvar investigated and determined that the error stemmed from editing the original questionnaire instead of creating a fresh one. He provided screenshots and a detailed example showing how the update altered the record’s timestamp. Sarah Emerson notified Leigh and Mikayla of the correct workflow: create a new questionnaire for each admission and avoid editing older entries. The ticket was marked closed after this clarification, with the knowledge base updated by Luke Evers to prevent future recurrence. No system changes were required; the solution was purely procedural.

**Additional Information** 
- The issue involved only one user and one patient. 
- Non‑live environment test on Scratch could not reproduce the problem because the data source was missing. 
- The conversation moved from Emerson to Dianne Chagaris, then to ANZ support, before Jeff Malvar resolved the case. 
- The knowledge base entry by Luke Evers indicates the ticket was re‑closed with thanks. 
- Attachments: “Admission Assessment problem.docx” and Jeff’s explanatory documents. 
- Severity level remains low; however, the procedural change will mitigate similar incidents.

Summary: T2023.6 BASE - Impossible d'enregistrer un CCMU GEMSA
Description:
**Problem** 
Caroline Dachary reported an error when attempting to process an episode in the Med‑URG “Menu Dpi” workflow. The attached document “T2023.6 Erreur CCMU GEMSA.doc” shows the error message that appears for several episodes. Initial attempts to reproduce the problem on internal test environments failed, but it reproduced on a live episode outside of Urgence on the LIVE environment. The error occurs for episodes that are not present in the CCOM TRN database, leading to a message that the episode cannot be processed.

**Solution** 
Khadijetou Boujoumaa modified the parameter configuration for the BASE system (where web services do not pass through Ensemble) and requested a new emergency episode be created on BASE. Caroline created patient IPP 24006572 with episode 24078189, which initially still produced the error. After the configuration change, the error was no longer displayed for the newly admitted patient. Boujoumaa then investigated two other problematic episodes (24066674 & 24066684) and confirmed that they do not exist in CCOM TRN, explaining the error as expected behavior. 
In reference to ticket I435719, Boujoumaa cancelled episode 24066684, re‑admitted the patient to Urgence, and verified that a new episode 24078190 was correctly created in Trak and Clinicom and that the CCMU GEMSA record was successfully registered. The conclusion is that the error is triggered only when an episode is missing from CCOM TRN; once the episode is properly created and present, the workflow succeeds.

**Additional Information** 
- The issue is specific to BASE environments where web services bypass Ensemble. 
- The error message is considered normal when an episode is absent from CCOM TRN. 
- After configuration adjustments and validation against live data, no further errors were observed for the newly created patient. 
- The ticket was closed by Boujoumaa after confirming that all reported issues had been addressed.

Summary: GXXXMUST questionnaire in LIVE
Description:
**Problem** 
Ewelina Dubij reported that the text representation (Text Rep) for the GXXXMUST questionnaire only displays the total score instead of the full questionnaire content. This omission affects legal record-keeping. The issue was first logged by Dubij and quickly escalated to UK support and then to regional 2nd line support. Initial investigation confirmed the problem in the Edition environment and the ticket was transferred to Bree Parker for further review.

**Solution** 
Bree Parker replicated the issue in Edition, then added a Text Rep at the site level as a temporary fix, first in a non‑live environment (BASE) for testing. She confirmed the GXXXMUST questionnaire is used in four encounter entry types: COVID‑19 Assessment, Medical Admission, Pre‑Operative Assessment, and Nursing Inpatient Admission, all within the ENXX Nurses Outpatient and ENXX Nurses Ward IP contexts. 
During testing in BASE, Dubij noted that height and weight were missing from the Text Rep. Bree suspected that these data are entered via the thermometer rather than the questionnaire and therefore may not be captured. She removed height and weight from the Text Rep and later raised a JIRA ticket to implement a conditional expression if those values need to be displayed. 
Dubij also identified an unnecessary text block highlighted in yellow; Bree removed this text and confirmed the display was correct. After verifying the changes in BASE, Bree progressed the configuration change request (CCR) through TEST to LIVE. Dubij verified the Text Rep in the LIVE environment, confirming that the full questionnaire now appears correctly, with the score and all relevant fields.

**Additional Information** 
- The ticket was initially prioritized by the reporter and moved through multiple support tiers. 
- Key communication steps: initial acknowledgement by Dianne (UK Support), follow‑up questions by David Waits (live vs non‑live), transfer to Bree Parker, and iterative testing and updates. 
- All modifications were carried out in non‑live (BASE), then tested (TEST), and finally deployed (LIVE). 
- The final resolution involved: adding a Text Rep at the site level, removing height/weight from the Text Rep, removing extraneous text, and raising a JIRA for future conditional display of height/weight if required. 
- The ticket was closed by Dubij after confirming the live environment displayed as expected.

Summary: questionnaire demande de transport
Description:
**Problem** 
After the latest update, transport‑request questionnaires (CERFA “bon de transport”) are erroneously displayed on every episode in the patient summary and on the diagnostics tab of the dietitians’ module (e.g., IPP1209639). The display is caused by questionnaires that have an end date, are marked “Obsolète”, and are linked to a questionnaire group that also has an end date. The group link’s end date forces the questionnaire to remain visible on all charts, even after the questionnaire itself is disabled. Users report that this clutter interferes with their workflow. The issue was identified in several environments (FE 38, Base) and was linked to ticket numbers TC‑308563, TC‑402446, and TC‑416580.

**Solution** 
Experts confirmed that the correct fix is to remove the end date from the link between the questionnaire FRXXIP0019 and its group “Formulaire CERFA.” By doing so, the link stays active even when the questionnaire is inactive, allowing historical entries to remain visible in the correct location. The same rule applies to all questionnaires that have an end date, are tagged “Obsolète,” and are linked to a questionnaire group: the group link must not have an end date. This change prevents obsolete transport questionnaires from appearing on new episodes while preserving access to past entries. The temporary workaround of re‑activating the link was replaced by the permanent removal of the end date.

**Additional Information** 
- The ticket was opened by Gaëlle PETIT, with input from Sandy Kromer and Jean‑Luc Boulan. 
- Gary Pemberton and Enrica Fossa handled initial support and transferred the issue to French support. 
- Khadijetou Boujoumaa led the investigation, performed the interim fix, and coordinated with experts for validation. 
- The fix was applied and the ticket was marked resolved after confirmation from Sandy Kromer. 
- No further action items remain; the ticket is closed.

Caroline Dachary reported that deleting a questionnaire makes it impossible to create a new one. This questionnaire is mandatory for the upcoming release, so the lack of a new instance blocks the rollout. The current workaround works in the live environment but cannot be applied in the current version, as documented in the attached “CCMU GEMSA Motif de suppression renseigné par erreur.doc.” The ticket was escalated to French support and a Jira issue (TC‑407157) has been created, linked to ticket PC-Q0016.

**Solution** 
The live workaround involves manipulating the error motive to unblock the questionnaire: 
1. Change the error motive – the “Apply” and “Save” buttons become enabled. 
2. Clear the error motive and click “Save.” 
3. Have an urgentist complete the CCMU/GEMSA form using a *correction* motive (not the error motive) and then perform the release. 

This sequence cannot be replicated on the BASE environment. To address the root problem, the product team will modify the script so that a user can create a new questionnaire instance if the previous instance was deleted. The script change is tracked in Jira TC‑407157 and will be evaluated on 19/04. The necessary patch (T2024.3.20240802.0315.FRXX.PATCH.IRIS2022.B125) has already been released in the PC-Q0016 tracking and includes the fix. Once applied, the issue should be resolved, eliminating the need for the current workaround.

**Additional Information** 
- The live version in use is T2023.6; the patch that contains the fix is T2024.3.20240802.0315.FRXX.PATCH.IRIS2022.B125. 
- The ticket has been closed after the patch deployment, as confirmed by Khadijetou Boujoumaa. 
- The workaround steps were provided by Caroline in a live‑only context and cannot be transferred to the BASE environment. 
- The Jira issue TC‑407157 is the formal tracking of the script modification; PC-Q0016 serves as the parent ticket for this support case. 
- No further actions are pending; the ticket is now considered resolved.

Summary: Questionnaire discrepancies between environments
Description:
**Problem** 
The requested update to the questionnaire layout was being promoted from the Base environment to Test with an intention to deploy to Live that evening. The layout change did not appear in Test, causing a critical failure for users in the Live environment. Justin Craig, representing Golden Jubilee NHS, raised the issue as a P1 priority, citing ongoing Live issues that the change was supposed to resolve. Subsequent communication revealed an XML error when regenerating the questionnaire, further preventing the change from applying successfully. The incident was initially categorized as a Crisis, then downgraded to Urgent by Mark Ryan after reviewing the appropriate priority level. The issue was labeled as a “partially down” outage with severe clinical and administrative impact affecting all users. A screenshot of the Live questionnaire error and the CCR document CCR1094.docx were attached. Contact details were shared (Mark’s phone 07748108087), and a Teams call was scheduled to troubleshoot.

**Solution** 
Mark Ryan assumed responsibility, moving the ticket to himself and into an Investigation status. He exported the preference from Base, loaded it into Test, and confirmed the change was present there. The priority was set to Urgent. When the issue persisted, Ryan performed a rollback of the Live change, re‑applied the Test configuration (SCGJ1094) to Live, and verified that the questionnaire generated correctly with no further errors. He monitored the system from 06:00 the next day, awaiting user confirmation. Justin Craig confirmed that the layout appeared correct in Live, and that end users would verify satisfaction tomorrow. With user approval, Ryan marked the ticket Resolved and instructed Justin to close the case. No further technical actions were required.

**Additional Information** 
- Ticket ID: Not explicitly provided, referenced as “CCR1094.docx” and “SCGJ1094”. 
- Stakeholders: Justin Craig (Application Specialist, Golden Jubilee NHS), Mark Ryan (Support Engineer), Gary Pemberton (UK Support Team), Mike Bresnahan (UK Support Team), Debbie Falce (linking CCR). 
- Priority changes: P1 (Crisis) → Urgent → Crisis (temporary) → Urgent. 
- Communication methods: Email, iService ticket system, phone, Teams call. 
- Key documents: CCR1094.docx, Live questionnaire error screenshot. 
- Contact: Mark Ryan’s mobile 07748108087. 
- Timeline: Incident raised on April 17, 2024, with final resolution confirmed after 07:00 the following morning.
